title: Modular Arithmetic and Public-Key Reasoning · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A product-security pair incrementally builds bounded modular arithmetic reasoning notebook, changes one attacker-controlled input, and keeps earlier Go, accessibility, secret-lifecycle, testing, and evidence invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of bounded modular arithmetic reasoning notebook while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compute and explain modular equivalence, reduction, addition, multiplication, exponentiation, and negative representatives.
Use Euclid and extended Euclid to determine coprimality and modular inverses and identify non-invertible cases.
Trace square-and-multiply cost and state why secret exponent code must use vetted constant-time implementations.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Crypto Go Modular Congruence
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop cryptographic engineering team handles case 5170 during a credential migration window. Build deterministic Go evidence to normalize modular values and distinguish mathematical correctness from approved constant-time group execution; cap bytes, work, memory, time, attempts, concurrency, and retained secret material before use; then remove one transfer gate and state exactly which production claim is no longer supported. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compute and explain modular equivalence, reduction, addition, multiplication, exponentiation, and negative representatives.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Crypto Go Modular Congruence.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compute and explain modular equivalence, reduction, addition, multiplication, exponentiation, and negative representatives. For Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Crypto Go Modular Congruence, keep Crypto Go Modular Congruence connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: For Cryptography and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Crypto Go Toy Math Separation, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Crypto Go Toy Math Separation precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Cryptography and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Crypto Go Toy Math Separation, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A workshop cryptographic engineering team handles case 2837 during a signed release review. Build deterministic Go evidence to normalize modular values and distinguish mathematical correctness from approved constant-time group execution; reject toy, obsolete, unauthenticated, self-signed-trust, downgrade, and security-by-obscurity shortcuts; then remove one transfer gate and state exactly which production claim is no longer supported. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Keep small-number demonstrations outside production code and name the parameter, randomness, validation, and side-channel evidence they omit.

For Cryptography and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Crypto Go Toy Math Separation, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A toy RSA example is production-ready after increasing the integer values.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Crypto Go Toy Math Separation
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop cryptographic engineering team handles case 1218 during a replicated device restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to normalize modular values and distinguish mathematical correctness from approved constant-time group execution; reject toy, obsolete, unauthenticated, self-signed-trust, downgrade, and security-by-obscurity shortcuts; then change one authenticated byte and prove clean rejection without partial effect. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Keep small-number demonstrations outside production code and name the parameter, randomness, validation, and side-channel evidence they omit.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one deterministic Crypto Go Toy Math Separation model, omit canonical bytes, asset, adversary, key and nonce ownership, tamper rejection, secret-safe failure, and native transfer gates, then call the browser result cryptographically secure.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Crypto Go Toy Math Separation condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
